key_insight: The paper shows that bilevel optimization with a strongly convex lower level can still be solved to second-order stationarity efficiently using a deterministic single-loop cubic regularized Newton method with optimal O(^{-1.5}) oracle complexity.
engineering_relevance: This gives a practical route to faster hyperparameter tuning, meta-learning, and design problems where nested optimization must be solved reliably at second-order accuracy.
method: Builds a double-loop CRN baseline to establish the optimal outer convergence rate.
Introduces a single-loop algorithm combining one lower-level gradient step with one Newton step for hypergradient computation.
Proves deterministic O(^{-1.5}) total oracle complexity for finding an -SOSP of the hyperfunction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a theoretical convergence and complexity analysis, including an optimal O(^{-1.5}) rate for the proposed single-loop method.
watch_item: The convergence guarantee relies on carefully controlled hypergradient and hyper-Hessian approximations; seemingly simple algorithmic modifications may break the proof and performance.
paper_title: On Second-Order Methods for Bilevel Optimization
